overview: Proposed solution to synchronize session state across the React frontend, Fastify Node.js services, and FastAPI Python services to ensure polyglot parity in auth resolution.

inScope: Unified JWT validation across all microservices
outOfScope: Migration of existing legacy user database
successMetrics: 0% mismatch in session resolution between Node and Python backends

architecture.pattern: API Gateway with Shared Redis Cache
architecture.description: All incoming requests hit a unified API gateway. Token validation is performed via a shared Redis cache accessible by both Node.js and Python ecosystems.

designDecisions.Cache Selection: Redis chosen over Memcached for native pub/sub support on token revocation.

flows.name: Token Revocation Event
flows.description: User logs out, triggering a global session kill.
steps: Downstream services clear local memory cache

risks.description: Redis cluster failure causes auth bottlenecks
risks.mitigationStrategy: Deploy Redis in Multi-AZ configuration with automatic failover.

phases.name: Phase 1: Shadow Validation
successCriteria: Logs show 100% agreement between old and new validation logic

rolloutPlan.rollbackProcedure: Revert API Gateway routing rules to bypass the new validation middleware via emergency GitHub Action workflow.
